title: Visibility: public, private, protected
description: Control access to properties and methods.

slides.title: Access Modifiers
slides.content:
# Public, Private, Protected

PHP supports three visibility keywords:

*   **public**: accessible from anywhere (inside class, derived classes, and outside).
*   **protected**: accessible within the class itself and by inheriting classes, but not from outside.
*   **private**: accessible only within the class that defines them. Not even in child classes.

This is part of **encapsulation** – hiding internal details and exposing only what's necessary.

slides.title: Visibility Example
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
class Person {
    public $name;
    protected $age;
    private $ssn;
    public function __construct($name, $age, $ssn) {
        $this->name = $name;
        $this->age = $age;
        $this->ssn = $ssn;
    }
}
$p = new Person('Alice', 30, '123-45-6789');
echo $p->name;
?>

quizData.question: A property declared as protected can be accessed from:

options.text: Within the class and its descendants

quizData.explanation: protected allows access within the class itself and any child classes.
